campaign: RTSC no-cooling flat-band-at-E_F — CeCo3B2-type 4d/5d-kagome family sweep (triangulation v3 lane)
probe: structural-sibling flat-band gate-check on the FIXED LaRu3Si2 relaxed cell (a=5.7175 Ang, c/a=0.62496); element substitution only (Ru -> Os / Rh), no per-sibling vc-relax
method: QE 7.5 scf (nspin=2, smearing mp 0.02, ecut 90/360 Ry, SG15 ONCV PBE-1.2) -> bands Gamma-K-M-Gamma-A-L-H crystal_b (verbosity='high' REQUIRED: >=100 k-points else QE suppresses per-k eigenvalue stdout) -> parse_flatband.py (flat band = min-dispersion band within 1.5 eV of E_F). 482 k-points parsed per sibling.
gate_definition: PRE-REGISTERED (c9, no goalpost move): PASS |dE|<0.10 AND m<0.5; FALSIFY dE>0.2 OR m>0.5; INCONCLUSIVE 0.1<=|dE|<=0.2 AND m<0.5. dE = E_flatband_mean - E_F.
honest_caveat: 1st-pass SCREEN on the FIXED LaRu3Si2 lattice (siblings NOT individually vc-relaxed) -> dE is APPROXIMATE (the true relaxed-cell dE may shift by ~0.05-0.1 eV as the lattice adjusts to Os/Rh ionic radii). A GREEN here is a PROMOTE-to-vc-relax signal, not a closed positive. Magnetism (m) is robust (scf nspin=2 on the fixed cell is a fair magnetic test). This is a DESIGN gate (flat-band-at-E_F), NOT a room-temp Tc claim.

siblings.substitution: Ru 4d7 -> Os 5d7 (isoelectronic, heavier 5d / stronger SOC, same d-count)
siblings.interpretation: BOTH conditions met: |dE|=0.0392 eV < 0.10 (flat band sits 39 meV ABOVE E_F) AND m=0.00 (non-magnetic). The 5d Os sibling lands the kagome flat band even CLOSER to E_F than LaRu3Si2 itself (|dE| 0.039 vs 0.055). Confirms the triangulation-v3 prediction: the CeCo3B2-type d7 kagome (4d OR 5d) intrinsically places the flat band at E_F. PROMOTE to per-sibling vc-relax + DFPT lambda/Tc.

siblings.substitution: Ru 4d7 -> Rh 4d8 (one extra d-electron, same 4d row)
siblings.interpretation: FALSIFY: dE=+0.260 eV > 0.2 (flat band sits 260 meV ABOVE E_F) despite m=0.00 (non-magnetic). The extra d-electron (d8 vs d7) raises E_F into the dispersive manifold while the flat band stays put -> the band is now well above E_F. Brackets the fill direction: d7 (Ru/Os) = winner, d8 (Rh) = overshoots. The d-count is the chemistry lever, consistent with triangulation v3.

finding: d-count brackets the flat-band alignment in the CeCo3B2-type kagome: d7 (Ru 4d, Os 5d) lands the flat band within 0.06 eV of E_F (both GREEN), d8 (Rh) overshoots to +0.26 eV (RED). LaOs3Si2 is a NEW non-magnetic flat-band-at-E_F winner (2nd after LaRu3Si2), and the 5d row works as well as 4d. The triangulation-v3 'sweep CeCo3B2-type 4d/5d-kagome family' lane is validated: 1 new GREEN + 1 informative RED that brackets the fill axis.
host: summer free pool (6 cores, mpirun -np 6, OMP=1)
root_cause_note: bands eigenvalue stdout was suppressed across two earlier attempts (mistakenly blamed on disk_io='none'); REAL cause = QE prints 'Number of k-points >= 100: set verbosity=high to print the bands' and omits the per-k blocks by default. Fix = verbosity='high' in bands.in &control. Also added self-logging (exec >> log) to survive setsid detachment.
